message.content: show examples of API gateway SAM templates in this code

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- datalake-ci-permissions: Use when user asks to add or update any AWS resource deployment or GitLab CI IAM roles scoped to the datalake environment. Do not trigger for non-datalake environments (dev, prod, sandbox, services) unless explicitly requested.
- mr-description: Writes merge request message description when raising a GitLab Merge Request. Use when creating a merge request or when user asks to summarise changes for a merge request.
- commit-message: Generate concise git commit messages for Data Engineering changes. Use PROACTIVELY when the user asks to commit files, create a commit, or generate a git commit message.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
2	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
3	Description: DNS API Ingestion
4	
5	Parameters:
6	  Environment:
7	    Description: Environment
8	    Type: String
9	  SupermetricsSecret:
10	    Description: Secret name for Supermetrics API key
11	    Type: String
12	  PropertySecret:
13	    Description: Secret name for Property API key
14	    Type: String
15	  DeCommonLayer:
16	    Description: ARN of dns-de-common-layer
17	    Type: String
18	  MriCredentialSecret:
19	    Description: Secret name for MRI credentials
20	    Type: String
21	  ShopworksTokenSSMParameter:
22	    Description: SSM Parameter to store Bearer token for Shopworks API endpoints
23	    Type: String
24	  HelloCustomerTokenSSMParameter:
25	    Description: SSM Parameter to store Bearer token for HelloCustomer API endpoints
26	    Type: String
27	  HelloCustomerClientCredentialSSMParameter:
28	    Description: SSM Parameter to store client credentails for HelloCustomer API
29	    Type: String
30	  EventBus:
31	    Description: Name of the Supernova Eventbridge bus
32	    Type: String
33	  TeamName:
34	    Description: Team responsible for application
35	    Type: String
36	    Default: de
37	
38	Resources:
39	  ServerlessAccessManager:
40	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
41	    Properties:
42	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
43	      FunctionName: !Sub 'dns-de-api-ingestor-${Environment}'
44	      VpcExecution: 'true'
45	      Targets:
46	        DevTargetS3:
47	          Type: S3
48	          Properties:
49	            Bucket: hbi-dns-dev-rdl-staging
50	        ProdTargetS3:
51	          Type: S3
52	          Properties:
53	            Bucket: hbi-dns-rdl-staging
54	        Supernova:
55	          Type: EventBridge
56	          Properties:
57	            Bus: !Ref EventBus
58	
59	  SecretsSSMPermsPolicy:
60	    Type: AWS::IAM::Policy
61	    Properties:
62	      PolicyName: api-ingest-secret-ssm-perms
63	      PolicyDocument:
64	        Version: "2012-10-17"
65	        Statement:
66	          - Effect: Allow
67	            Action:
68	              - 'secretsmanager:DescribeSecret'
69	              - 'secretsmanager:GetSecretValue'
70	            Resource: 
71	              - !Ref SupermetricsSecret
72	              - !Ref PropertySecret
73	              - !Ref MriCredentialSecret
74	          - Effect: Allow
75	            Action: 'ssm:GetParameter'
76	            Resource: !Sub arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:parameter/dns/hello_customer/*
77	          - Effect: Allow
78	            Action: 'ssm:PutParameter'
79	            Resource: !Sub arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:parameter/dns/hello_customer/token_*
80	      Roles:
81	        - !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName
82	
83	  APIIngestionLambdaFunction:
84	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
85	    Properties:
86	      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
87	      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
88	      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
89	      Layers:
90	        - !Ref DeCommonLayer
91	      Environment:
92	        Variables:
93	          Supermetrics_API_Key: !Ref SupermetricsSecret
94	          Property_API_Key: !Ref PropertySecret
95	          MRI_credential: !Ref MriCredentialSecret
96	          Shopworks_token: !Sub '{{resolve:ssm:${ShopworksTokenSSMParameter}}}'
97	          HelloCustomer_token_Param: !Ref HelloCustomerTokenSSMParameter
98	          HelloCustomer_clientcreds_Param: !Ref HelloCustomerClientCredentialSSMParameter
99	          env: !Sub '${Environment}'
100	      Tags:
101	        application: 'dns-api-ingestion'
102	        created_by: cloudformation
103	        owner: 'de'
104	        team: !Ref TeamName
105	      CodeUri: src/
106	      Handler: api_client.lambda_handler
107	      Runtime: python3.13
108	      MemorySize: 10240
109	      Timeout: 900
110	      EphemeralStorage:
111	        Size: 10240
112	
113	
114	Outputs:
115	  APIIngestionLambdaFunctionARN:
116	    Description: "API ingestion lambda function ARN"
117	    Value: !GetAtt APIIngestionLambdaFunction.Arn
118	
119

file.content:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: DNS API Ingestion

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  SupermetricsSecret:
    Description: Secret name for Supermetrics API key
    Type: String
  PropertySecret:
    Description: Secret name for Property API key
    Type: String
  DeCommonLayer:
    Description: ARN of dns-de-common-layer
    Type: String
  MriCredentialSecret:
    Description: Secret name for MRI credentials
    Type: String
  ShopworksTokenSSMParameter:
    Description: SSM Parameter to store Bearer token for Shopworks API endpoints
    Type: String
  HelloCustomerTokenSSMParameter:
    Description: SSM Parameter to store Bearer token for HelloCustomer API endpoints
    Type: String
  HelloCustomerClientCredentialSSMParameter:
    Description: SSM Parameter to store client credentails for HelloCustomer API
    Type: String
  EventBus:
    Description: Name of the Supernova Eventbridge bus
    Type: String
  TeamName:
    Description: Team responsible for application
    Type: String
    Default: de

Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'dns-de-api-ingestor-${Environment}'
      VpcExecution: 'true'
      Targets:
        DevTargetS3:
          Type: S3
          Properties:
            Bucket: hbi-dns-dev-rdl-staging
        ProdTargetS3:
          Type: S3
          Properties:
            Bucket: hbi-dns-rdl-staging
        Supernova:
          Type: EventBridge
          Properties:
            Bus: !Ref EventBus

  SecretsSSMPermsPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Policy
    Properties:
      PolicyName: api-ingest-secret-ssm-perms
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - 'secretsmanager:DescribeSecret'
              - 'secretsmanager:GetSecretValue'
            Resource: 
              - !Ref SupermetricsSecret
              - !Ref PropertySecret
              - !Ref MriCredentialSecret
          - Effect: Allow
            Action: 'ssm:GetParameter'
            Resource: !Sub arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:parameter/dns/hello_customer/*
          - Effect: Allow
            Action: 'ssm:PutParameter'
            Resource: !Sub arn:aws:ssm:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:parameter/dns/hello_customer/token_*
      Roles:
        - !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleName

  APIIngestionLambdaFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      VpcConfig: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.VpcConfig
      Layers:
        - !Ref DeCommonLayer
      Environment:
        Variables:
          Supermetrics_API_Key: !Ref SupermetricsSecret
          Property_API_Key: !Ref PropertySecret
          MRI_credential: !Ref MriCredentialSecret
          Shopworks_token: !Sub '{{resolve:ssm:${ShopworksTokenSSMParameter}}}'
          HelloCustomer_token_Param: !Ref HelloCustomerTokenSSMParameter
          HelloCustomer_clientcreds_Param: !Ref HelloCustomerClientCredentialSSMParameter
          env: !Sub '${Environment}'
      Tags:
        application: 'dns-api-ingestion'
        created_by: cloudformation
        owner: 'de'
        team: !Ref TeamName
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: api_client.lambda_handler
      Runtime: python3.13
      MemorySize: 10240
      Timeout: 900
      EphemeralStorage:
        Size: 10240


Outputs:
  APIIngestionLambdaFunctionARN:
    Description: "API ingestion lambda function ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt APIIngestionLambdaFunction.Arn

content.content:
1	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
2	Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
3	Description: Realtime Product Recommendations API
4	
5	Parameters:
6	  Environment:
7	    Description: Environment
8	    Type: String
9	  ImageTag:
10	    Description: "Image SHA"
11	    Type: String
12	  LogLevel:
13	    Description: Log level can be DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR or CRITICAL
14	    Type: String
15	    Default: 'INFO'
16	  DynamoDBTable:
17	    Description: Name of the DynamoDB table to read affinity-events from
18	    Type: String
19	  NumberOfSkusToReturn:
20	    Description: Number of SKUs that should be returned as product recommendations
21	    Type: Number
22	    Default: 10
23	  NumberOfEventsToQuery:
24	    Description: Max number of affinity-events that should be queried from the DynamoDB table per session ID. Prevents slow performance or out-of-memory crash.
25	    Type: Number
26	    Default: 10
27	  ListenerRuleArn:
28	    Description: ARN of the Load-balancer-listener-rule that the realtime-product-recommendations API will be exposed behind
29	    Type: String
30	  ListenerRulePriority:
31	    Description: Load balancer rule priority, used so that different dev versions can coexist in the same load balancer by using different listener rule priorities.
32	    Type: Number
33	  ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum:
34	    Type: Number
35	    Default: 1  # Minimum provisioned concurrency cost of 1x 1769MB lambda instance = $20.80 / month
36	  ProvisionedConcurrencyMaximum:
37	    Type: Number
38	    Default: 10  # In case these are left running at max, the cost estimate of running 10x 1769MB lambda instances = $208 / month
39	  ProvisionedConcurrencyDefault:
40	    Type: Number
41	    Default: 1
42	  TargetUtilization:
43	    Description: Provisioned concurrency is autoscaled to target this usage ratio of maximum-concurrent-requests / provisioned-concurrency.
44	    Type: Number
45	    Default: 0.20
46	
47	
48	Resources:
49	  ServerlessAccessManager:
50	    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
51	    Properties:
52	      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
53	      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-${Environment}'
54	      Targets:
55	        DynamoDB:
56	          Type: DynamoDB
57	          Properties:
58	            Table: !Ref DynamoDBTable
59	
60	  RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction:
61	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
62	    Properties:
63	      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
64	      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
65	      Environment:
66	        Variables:
67	          Env: !Ref Environment
68	          DynamoDBTable: !Ref DynamoDBTable
69	          NumberOfSkusToReturn: !Ref NumberOfSkusToReturn
70	          NumberOfEventsToQuery: !Ref NumberOfEventsToQuery
71	          LogLevel: !Ref LogLevel
72	      PackageType: Image
73	      ImageUri: !Sub "334512769709.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/data-de/realtime-product-recommendations-api/${Environment}:${ImageTag}"
74	      Architectures: [x86_64]
75	      MemorySize: 1769  # Minimum memory for which we still get 1 full vCPU
76	      Timeout: 60
77	
78	  # Publish an immutable version every deploy (Description changes => new Version resource)
79	  RealtimeProductRecommendationsVersion:
80	    Type: AWS::Lambda::Version
81	    DependsOn: RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
82	    Properties:
83	      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
84	      Description: !Sub "deploy-${Environment}-${ImageTag}"
85	
86	  # New alias per deploy (lets us fully warm the new alias before switching ALB over)
87	  RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias:
88	    Type: AWS::Lambda::Alias
89	    Properties:
90	      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
91	      FunctionVersion: !GetAtt RealtimeProductRecommendationsVersion.Version
92	      Name: !Sub "live-${ImageTag}"
93	      ProvisionedConcurrencyConfig:
94	        ProvisionedConcurrentExecutions: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyDefault
95	
96	  ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget:
97	    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalableTarget
98	    DependsOn:
99	      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
100	    Properties:
101	      ServiceNamespace: lambda
102	      ScalableDimension: lambda:function:ProvisionedConcurrency
103	      ResourceId: !Sub "function:${RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction}:live-${ImageTag}"
104	      MinCapacity: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum
105	      MaxCapacity: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMaximum
106	      RoleARN: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/aws-service-role/lambda.application-autoscaling.amazonaws.com/AWSServiceRoleForApplicationAutoScaling_LambdaConcurrency"
107	
108	  ProvisionedConcurrencyTargetTracking:
109	    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalingPolicy
110	    DependsOn:
111	      - ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget
112	    Properties:
113	      PolicyName: ProvisionedConcurrencyTargetTracking
114	      PolicyType: TargetTrackingScaling
115	      ScalingTargetId: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget
116	      TargetTrackingScalingPolicyConfiguration:
117	        PredefinedMetricSpecification:
118	          PredefinedMetricType: LambdaProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization
119	        TargetValue: !Ref TargetUtilization
120	        ScaleInCooldown: 180  # If we scale down, wait 3 minutes before trying to scale down again
121	        ScaleOutCooldown: 60  # If we scale up, give provisioned concurrency time to reprovision and then 8 seconds for our lambda to initialize
122	
123	  # CloudWatch alarm: fires ONLY when metric is missing. This is used to scale us down when there is absolutely no activity, such as in dev environments.
124	  # This is needed as the scaling policy only triggers when there is activity on the lambda: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/provisioned-concurrency.html
125	  PcNoDataAlarm:
126	    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
127	    DependsOn:
128	      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
129	    Properties:
130	      AlarmName: !Sub "data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-PcNoData-${Environment}"
131	      AlarmDescription: "No ProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization datapoints => scale provisioned concurrency to minimum"
132	      Namespace: AWS/Lambda
133	      MetricName: ProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization
134	      Dimensions:
135	        - Name: FunctionName
136	          Value: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
137	        - Name: Resource
138	          Value: !Sub "${RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction}:live-${ImageTag}"
139	      Statistic: Maximum
140	      Period: 120
141	      EvaluationPeriods: 1  # Trigger on 'no data' for 2 minutes. But use 1 period, so as to reset the alarm after any activity, so it can re-trigger.
142	      ComparisonOperator: LessThanThreshold
143	      Threshold: -1             # impossible value => only 'missing' can trigger the alarm
144	      TreatMissingData: missing # missing datapoints => INSUFFICIENT_DATA
145	      # Trigger Lambda specifically when alarm enters "no data" / INSUFFICIENT_DATA
146	      InsufficientDataActions:
147	        - !GetAtt ScaleToMinFunction.Arn
148	
149	  AllowAlarmInvokeScaleToMin:
150	    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
151	    Properties:
152	      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
153	      FunctionName: !Ref ScaleToMinFunction
154	      Principal: lambda.alarms.cloudwatch.amazonaws.com
155	      SourceArn: !GetAtt PcNoDataAlarm.Arn
156	
157	  ScaleToMinFunction:
158	    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
159	    Properties:
160	      FunctionName: !Sub "data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-scale-to-min-${Environment}"
161	      Role: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/realtime-recommendations-scale-to-zero"
162	      Runtime: python3.13
163	      Handler: index.handler
164	      Timeout: 30
165	      MemorySize: 128
166	      Environment:
167	        Variables:
168	          TARGET_FUNCTION_NAME: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
169	          TARGET_ALIAS: !Sub "live-${ImageTag}"
170	          MIN_PC: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum
171	      InlineCode: |
172	        import os, boto3
173	        lambda_client = boto3.client("lambda")
174	
175	        def handler(event, context):
176	          fn = os.environ["TARGET_FUNCTION_NAME"]
177	          alias = os.environ["TARGET_ALIAS"]
178	          min_pc = int(os.environ["MIN_PC"])
179	          # Set provisioned concurrency on alias to minimum
180	          lambda_client.put_provisioned_concurrency_config(
181	            FunctionName=fn,
182	            Qualifier=alias,
183	            ProvisionedConcurrentExecutions=min_pc,
184	          )
185	          return {"ok": True, "function": fn, "alias": alias, "min_pc": min_pc}
186	
187	  # Allow ALB to invoke the (new) Lambda alias
188	  LoadBalancerLambdaPermission:
189	    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
190	    DependsOn:
191	      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
192	    Properties:
193	      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
194	      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
195	      Principal: elasticloadbalancing.amazonaws.com
196	
197	  ServiceListenerTargetGroup:
198	    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup
199	    DependsOn:
200	      - LoadBalancerLambdaPermission
201	      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias  # don’t switch until ProvisionedConcurrency is ready, to prevent downtime during deployments
202	    Properties:
203	      Name: !Sub "realtime-product-recs-${Environment}"
204	      TargetType: lambda
205	      Targets:
206	        - Id: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
207	
208	  ServiceListenerRule:
209	    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::ListenerRule
210	    Properties:
211	      Actions:
212	        - Type: forward
213	          TargetGroupArn: !Ref ServiceListenerTargetGroup
214	      Conditions:
215	        - Field: http-request-method
216	          HttpRequestMethodConfig:
217	            Values: ['POST']
218	        - Field: path-pattern
219	          PathPatternConfig:
220	            Values: ['/realtime_product_recommendations']
221	      ListenerArn: !Ref ListenerRuleArn
222	      Priority: !Ref ListenerRulePriority
223	
224	#  # Share cross-account lambda invocation permissions to the dev and prod accounts
225	#  DevLambdaPermission:
226	#    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
227	#    Properties:
228	#      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
229	#      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction.Alias
230	#      Principal: "arn:aws:iam::448714108511:root"   # dev account
231	#
232	#  ProdLambdaPermission:
233	#    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
234	#    Properties:
235	#      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
236	#      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction.Alias
237	#      Principal: "arn:aws:iam::482520917892:root"   # prod account
238	
239	Outputs:
240	  RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunctionARN:
241	    Description: "Realtime product recommendations API lambda ARN"
242	    Value: !GetAtt RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction.Arn
243

file.content:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: Realtime Product Recommendations API

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Description: Environment
    Type: String
  ImageTag:
    Description: "Image SHA"
    Type: String
  LogLevel:
    Description: Log level can be DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR or CRITICAL
    Type: String
    Default: 'INFO'
  DynamoDBTable:
    Description: Name of the DynamoDB table to read affinity-events from
    Type: String
  NumberOfSkusToReturn:
    Description: Number of SKUs that should be returned as product recommendations
    Type: Number
    Default: 10
  NumberOfEventsToQuery:
    Description: Max number of affinity-events that should be queried from the DynamoDB table per session ID. Prevents slow performance or out-of-memory crash.
    Type: Number
    Default: 10
  ListenerRuleArn:
    Description: ARN of the Load-balancer-listener-rule that the realtime-product-recommendations API will be exposed behind
    Type: String
  ListenerRulePriority:
    Description: Load balancer rule priority, used so that different dev versions can coexist in the same load balancer by using different listener rule priorities.
    Type: Number
  ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum:
    Type: Number
    Default: 1  # Minimum provisioned concurrency cost of 1x 1769MB lambda instance = $20.80 / month
  ProvisionedConcurrencyMaximum:
    Type: Number
    Default: 10  # In case these are left running at max, the cost estimate of running 10x 1769MB lambda instances = $208 / month
  ProvisionedConcurrencyDefault:
    Type: Number
    Default: 1
  TargetUtilization:
    Description: Provisioned concurrency is autoscaled to target this usage ratio of maximum-concurrent-requests / provisioned-concurrency.
    Type: Number
    Default: 0.20


Resources:
  ServerlessAccessManager:
    Type: Custom::ServerlessAccessManager
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:eu-west-1:${AWS::AccountId}:function:paas-serverless-access-manager"
      FunctionName: !Sub 'data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-${Environment}'
      Targets:
        DynamoDB:
          Type: DynamoDB
          Properties:
            Table: !Ref DynamoDBTable

  RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.FunctionName
      Role: !GetAtt ServerlessAccessManager.LambdaExecutionRoleArn
      Environment:
        Variables:
          Env: !Ref Environment
          DynamoDBTable: !Ref DynamoDBTable
          NumberOfSkusToReturn: !Ref NumberOfSkusToReturn
          NumberOfEventsToQuery: !Ref NumberOfEventsToQuery
          LogLevel: !Ref LogLevel
      PackageType: Image
      ImageUri: !Sub "334512769709.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/data-de/realtime-product-recommendations-api/${Environment}:${ImageTag}"
      Architectures: [x86_64]
      MemorySize: 1769  # Minimum memory for which we still get 1 full vCPU
      Timeout: 60

  # Publish an immutable version every deploy (Description changes => new Version resource)
  RealtimeProductRecommendationsVersion:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Version
    DependsOn: RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
      Description: !Sub "deploy-${Environment}-${ImageTag}"

  # New alias per deploy (lets us fully warm the new alias before switching ALB over)
  RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Alias
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
      FunctionVersion: !GetAtt RealtimeProductRecommendationsVersion.Version
      Name: !Sub "live-${ImageTag}"
      ProvisionedConcurrencyConfig:
        ProvisionedConcurrentExecutions: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyDefault

  ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget:
    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalableTarget
    DependsOn:
      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
    Properties:
      ServiceNamespace: lambda
      ScalableDimension: lambda:function:ProvisionedConcurrency
      ResourceId: !Sub "function:${RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction}:live-${ImageTag}"
      MinCapacity: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum
      MaxCapacity: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMaximum
      RoleARN: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/aws-service-role/lambda.application-autoscaling.amazonaws.com/AWSServiceRoleForApplicationAutoScaling_LambdaConcurrency"

  ProvisionedConcurrencyTargetTracking:
    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalingPolicy
    DependsOn:
      - ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget
    Properties:
      PolicyName: ProvisionedConcurrencyTargetTracking
      PolicyType: TargetTrackingScaling
      ScalingTargetId: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyScalableTarget
      TargetTrackingScalingPolicyConfiguration:
        PredefinedMetricSpecification:
          PredefinedMetricType: LambdaProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization
        TargetValue: !Ref TargetUtilization
        ScaleInCooldown: 180  # If we scale down, wait 3 minutes before trying to scale down again
        ScaleOutCooldown: 60  # If we scale up, give provisioned concurrency time to reprovision and then 8 seconds for our lambda to initialize

  # CloudWatch alarm: fires ONLY when metric is missing. This is used to scale us down when there is absolutely no activity, such as in dev environments.
  # This is needed as the scaling policy only triggers when there is activity on the lambda: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/provisioned-concurrency.html
  PcNoDataAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    DependsOn:
      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub "data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-PcNoData-${Environment}"
      AlarmDescription: "No ProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization datapoints => scale provisioned concurrency to minimum"
      Namespace: AWS/Lambda
      MetricName: ProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization
      Dimensions:
        - Name: FunctionName
          Value: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
        - Name: Resource
          Value: !Sub "${RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction}:live-${ImageTag}"
      Statistic: Maximum
      Period: 120
      EvaluationPeriods: 1  # Trigger on 'no data' for 2 minutes. But use 1 period, so as to reset the alarm after any activity, so it can re-trigger.
      ComparisonOperator: LessThanThreshold
      Threshold: -1             # impossible value => only 'missing' can trigger the alarm
      TreatMissingData: missing # missing datapoints => INSUFFICIENT_DATA
      # Trigger Lambda specifically when alarm enters "no data" / INSUFFICIENT_DATA
      InsufficientDataActions:
        - !GetAtt ScaleToMinFunction.Arn

  AllowAlarmInvokeScaleToMin:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      FunctionName: !Ref ScaleToMinFunction
      Principal: lambda.alarms.cloudwatch.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !GetAtt PcNoDataAlarm.Arn

  ScaleToMinFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub "data-de-realtime-product-recommendations-scale-to-min-${Environment}"
      Role: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/realtime-recommendations-scale-to-zero"
      Runtime: python3.13
      Handler: index.handler
      Timeout: 30
      MemorySize: 128
      Environment:
        Variables:
          TARGET_FUNCTION_NAME: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction
          TARGET_ALIAS: !Sub "live-${ImageTag}"
          MIN_PC: !Ref ProvisionedConcurrencyMinimum
      InlineCode: |
        import os, boto3
        lambda_client = boto3.client("lambda")

        def handler(event, context):
          fn = os.environ["TARGET_FUNCTION_NAME"]
          alias = os.environ["TARGET_ALIAS"]
          min_pc = int(os.environ["MIN_PC"])
          # Set provisioned concurrency on alias to minimum
          lambda_client.put_provisioned_concurrency_config(
            FunctionName=fn,
            Qualifier=alias,
            ProvisionedConcurrentExecutions=min_pc,
          )
          return {"ok": True, "function": fn, "alias": alias, "min_pc": min_pc}

  # Allow ALB to invoke the (new) Lambda alias
  LoadBalancerLambdaPermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    DependsOn:
      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
    Properties:
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias
      Principal: elasticloadbalancing.amazonaws.com

  ServiceListenerTargetGroup:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup
    DependsOn:
      - LoadBalancerLambdaPermission
      - RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias  # don’t switch until ProvisionedConcurrency is ready, to prevent downtime during deployments
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "realtime-product-recs-${Environment}"
      TargetType: lambda
      Targets:
        - Id: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsAlias

  ServiceListenerRule:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::ListenerRule
    Properties:
      Actions:
        - Type: forward
          TargetGroupArn: !Ref ServiceListenerTargetGroup
      Conditions:
        - Field: http-request-method
          HttpRequestMethodConfig:
            Values: ['POST']
        - Field: path-pattern
          PathPatternConfig:
            Values: ['/realtime_product_recommendations']
      ListenerArn: !Ref ListenerRuleArn
      Priority: !Ref ListenerRulePriority

#  # Share cross-account lambda invocation permissions to the dev and prod accounts
#  DevLambdaPermission:
#    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
#    Properties:
#      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
#      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction.Alias
#      Principal: "arn:aws:iam::448714108511:root"   # dev account
#
#  ProdLambdaPermission:
#    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
#    Properties:
#      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
#      FunctionName: !Ref RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction.Alias
#      Principal: "arn:aws:iam::482520917892:root"   # prod account

Outputs:
  RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunctionARN:
    Description: "Realtime product recommendations API lambda ARN"
    Value: !GetAtt RealtimeProductRecommendationsFunction.Arn

content.thinking:
The codebase doesn't appear to have any API Gateway SAM templates. The templates I found use:
1. `ingest-api` - a Lambda function that *calls* external APIs (it's an "API ingestion" lambda, not an API Gateway-fronted lambda)
2. `realtime-product-recommendations-api` - uses an ALB (Application Load Balancer) with listener rules, not API Gateway

Let me search more broadly to confirm there are no API Gateway SAM templates.

content.text:
This codebase doesn't have any API Gateway SAM templates. The two "API" lambdas use different patterns:

- **`lambda/ingest-api`** — A Lambda that *calls* external APIs (Supermetrics, Shopworks, etc.), not exposed via API Gateway
- **`lambda/realtime-product-recommendations-api`** — Exposed via an **ALB (Application Load Balancer)** using `AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::ListenerRule` and `TargetGroup`, not API Gateway

The `realtime-product-recommendations-api` template is the closest thing to an HTTP API pattern in this repo — it uses ALB listener rules to route `POST /realtime_product_recommendations` to a Lambda alias with provisioned concurrency autoscaling. That would be the best reference if you're building something similar.

There are no `AWS::Serverless::Api`, `AWS::Serverless::HttpApi`, or `AWS::ApiGateway::*` resources anywhere in the codebase.
